intro: Goniopteris hatchii, also known as Hatch's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, contributing to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris hatchii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
